title: Dialect Portability, NoSQL Tradeoffs, Distribution, and Production Defense · SQL Performance Board
summary: Unfamiliar support and public-service datasets requiring model repair, query composition, mutation safety, transaction diagnosis, analytical SQL, plan evidence, parameterization, privacy, portability, and recovery decisions. Planned artifact: Produce a cumulative evidence packet across unfamiliar cases, performance tasks, explanations, and root-cause repairs. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Trace data through creation, processing, review, retention, retrieval, and defensible destruction.
Classify data purpose, owner, sensitivity, quality risk, retention, and downstream harm before storage.
Represent unknown, missing, inapplicable, and not-yet-known values without substituting misleading sentinels.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.sql:
-- sql-basics cumulative relational data portfolio.
-- Each increment declares grain, predicts cardinality or changed rows, runs in fresh browser SQLite,
-- reconciles results, tests changed data, and records PostgreSQL or standard-SQL boundaries.

SELECT sqlite_version() AS browser_sqlite_version;

steps.title: Plan the cumulative evidence run · Exam Data Lifecycle
steps.instruction:
Order the Data Lifecycle evidence cycle for this exam task before implementing it.

Evidence target: Plan the cumulative evidence run · Exam Data Lifecycle.
steps.why: Correct order keeps Data Lifecycle assumptions visible before execution and verifies its result against a boundary case afterward. For Plan the cumulative evidence run · Exam Data Lifecycle, keep Data Lifecycle connected to executable relational evidence.

content.text: Move a SQLite 3.49.1 exam artifact toward PostgreSQL 18.4 while preserving relational intent. Record one dialect difference, consistency boundary, restore signal, and changed case 3; justify any non-relational component. Competency probe: Trace data through creation, processing, review, retention, retrieval, and defensible destruction.

content.title: Ordering constraint
content.text: Order work so Data Lifecycle has a declared contract before execution and a boundary or counterexample after it.

Predict relational transformations, constraint or transaction effects, and dialect boundary before execution for the exam Data Lifecycle case
Run the smallest browser SQL statement and retain result rows, changed count, error, or query plan for the exam Data Lifecycle case
Reconcile totals and invariants, test changed data, and document security, recovery, and portability limits for the exam Data Lifecycle case

The record names the assumption, applies Capstone Defense, and reports a changed-case check tied to the stakeholder decision.

steps.title: Build the performance evidence · Exam Capstone Defense
steps.instruction:
Append a runnable SQLite increment headed "-- Evidence: sql-capstone-defense-78pvf5" with a concrete grain, hypothesis, or prediction; dialect, storage-model, distribution, or production evidence; and an observation, reconciliation, or verification tied to returned rows, changed rows, an error, or a plan.

Evidence target: Build the performance evidence · Exam Capstone Defense.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Capstone Defense into observable performance evidence. For Build the performance evidence · Exam Capstone Defense, keep Capstone Defense connected to executable relational evidence.

content.code:
-- Evidence: sql-capstone-defense-78pvf5
-- grain: declare the exact row or mutation grain before running
SELECT typeof(owner_id) AS sqlite_runtime_type, COUNT(*) AS rows
FROM tickets GROUP BY typeof(owner_id) ORDER BY sqlite_runtime_type;
-- observation: reconcile the returned rows, changed count, error, or plan with the prediction
content.caption: Shape of the required increment; replace generic language with the current stakeholder case.

content.title: Evidence is more than syntax
content.text: The Capstone Defense structural check is only one gate. a production-ready cross-dialect data system portfolio must also survive the stated changed case and causal explanation.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Defend schema, queries, mutations, transactions, security, performance, portability, recovery, and ethical limits under changed evidence.
